surface.name: Create an OCI Free Trial tenancy and connect to the first Linux instance
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Selected route: Create an OCI Free Trial tenancy and connect to the first Linux instance. OCI lists several coequal first resources, but its official Launching Your First Linux Instance tutorial supplies a complete console path from an OCI account through compartment and network creation to an authenticated shell on an Oracle Linux VM. Prior surface before policy commit: OCI resource type and interface not selected.

alternatives_considered.name: Launch your first Linux instance
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This is one compute-specific choice among several first-resource tutorials.

alternatives_considered.name: Get Started with the CLI
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: This interface-specific walkthrough creates compartments, networks, and instances and requires separate CLI setup; OCI does not present it as the only general route.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a first OCI resource without a preselected service or interface.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery.
No OCI service, region-specific resource, compartment, or interface has been selected.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: The selected Free Trial route demonstrates first success when the developer is connected to the default shell for the first Linux instance.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer is connected to the default shell for the instance.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The developer is connected to the default shell for the instance.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The developer is connected to the default shell for the instance.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Selected under workshop fastest/most-used policy; terminal taken from the committed official candidate route.

prerequisites.requirement: Free Trial signup requires country or territory, name, a valid email address, CAPTCHA completion, address, and phone details.
Most users must provide an accepted payment card for identity verification; Oracle may place a small temporary hold that is removed automatically.
A Home Region, resource type, and interaction interface must be selected. The Home Region cannot be changed after account creation.

primary_path.action: Open Oracle Cloud Free Trial signup, enter country or territory, name, valid email, and CAPTCHA, then select Verify my email.
details: Oracle sends a verification link to the supplied address.
The link is valid for 30 minutes.
primary_path.input: Country, name, email address, and CAPTCHA response.
primary_path.success_signal: The verification email arrives with a signup link.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An expired link requires restarting email verification; some countries must contact Oracle Sales instead of self-service signup.

primary_path.action: Select the Home Region, accept the terms, and continue.
details: The Home Region determines where the tenancy is created and cannot be changed later.
Free-tier availability varies by region.
primary_path.success_signal: The address and phone section opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A mistaken Home Region cannot be corrected after account creation.

primary_path.action: Enter address and phone details, then add an accepted credit or eligible debit card and select Finish.
details: Oracle uses the card for identity verification and may place a small temporary hold.
The Free Trial does not charge the card unless the account is upgraded.
primary_path.success_signal: The payment verification finishes and the trial terms are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An unsupported card or failed verification blocks signup.

primary_path.action: Accept the trial terms and select Start my free trial.
details: The promotion includes $300 in credits valid for up to 30 days.
primary_path.input: Completed signup form and accepted terms.
primary_path.success_signal: Oracle begins provisioning cloud services in the tenancy.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Provisioning must finish before Console access is available.

primary_path.action: Wait for tenancy provisioning and the redirect to the OCI Console, then retain the welcome email.
details: Oracle redirects the developer after the services are provisioned and also sends a welcome email.
primary_path.output: Provisioned OCI tenancy and authenticated Console session.
primary_path.success_signal: The OCI Console opens and the welcome email confirms the account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The documentation does not state a provisioning duration.

primary_path.action: Open the OCI documentation home and follow its Start route to first-resource tutorials.
details: The home distinguishes Start from Develop routes.
The first-resource index groups tutorials by resource type.
primary_path.input: Provisioned OCI tenancy and intent to create a resource.
primary_path.output: A menu of official resource-specific tutorials.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer reaches the documented first-resource choices.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No wait is documented at the tutorial router.

primary_path.action: Select compute, storage, networking, database, load balancing, or another resource path and its interface.
details: OCI resources may be region-specific or availability-domain-specific.
The CLI route itself includes compartment, VCN, and instance decisions.
primary_path.input: A human-selected resource outcome and interface.
primary_path.success_signal: No common platform-level terminal signal is documented.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The journey cannot continue as one canonical path without the selection.

primary_path.action: Open Oracle Cloud Free Trial signup, enter country or territory, name, valid email, and CAPTCHA, then select Verify my email.
details: Oracle sends a verification link that is valid for 30 minutes.
primary_path.input: Country, name, reachable email, and CAPTCHA response.
primary_path.success_signal: The verification email arrives with a signup link.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An expired link requires restarting verification; some countries require contacting Oracle Sales.

primary_path.action: Open the verification link, create and confirm the password, optionally enter a company name, and choose a cloud account name.
primary_path.input: Valid email link, password, and cloud account name.
primary_path.success_signal: The signup form accepts the account information.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Password or cloud account name validation can block continuation.

primary_path.action: Select the Home Region, accept the terms, and continue.
details: The Home Region cannot be changed after account creation and Free Tier availability varies by region.
primary_path.success_signal: The address and phone section opens.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A mistaken Home Region cannot be corrected after the account is created.

primary_path.action: Enter the address and phone details, provide a post office box if Oracle asks for one, provide a CPF if the address is in Brazil, and select Continue.
details: The post office box and Brazil CPF fields are conditional requirements documented in the official signup instructions.
primary_path.input: Address, phone, and any requested post office box or Brazil CPF details.
primary_path.output: Accepted contact details and the payment verification section.
primary_path.success_signal: The Add payment verification method control becomes available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing or invalid address, phone, requested post office box, or Brazil CPF details blocks continuation.

primary_path.action: Select Add payment verification method, choose Credit Card, enter an accepted card's details, and select Finish.
details: Oracle uses the card for identity verification and may place a small temporary hold that is removed automatically.
primary_path.success_signal: Payment verification completes and the trial terms become available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An unsupported card or failed verification blocks signup.

primary_path.action: Accept the trial terms and select Start my free trial.
details: The promotion includes $300 in credits valid for up to 30 days.
primary_path.success_signal: Oracle begins provisioning cloud services.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Console access is unavailable until provisioning finishes.

primary_path.action: Wait for tenancy provisioning and the redirect to the OCI Console, then retain the welcome email.
details: Oracle does not state a provisioning duration on the signup page.
primary_path.output: Provisioned OCI tenancy and authenticated Console session.
primary_path.success_signal: The OCI Console opens and the welcome email confirms the account.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The resource tutorial cannot begin until provisioning completes.

primary_path.action: Confirm that the local macOS, Linux, or Windows computer has an `ssh` client.
details: The tutorial states that current versions of these operating systems include OpenSSH by default.
primary_path.success_signal: The `ssh` command is available locally.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Without an SSH client, the candidate cannot reach its terminal signal.

primary_path.action: Open Identity & Security, select Compartments, create a compartment with a name, required description, and parent compartment, then select it.
details: The tutorial uses one dedicated compartment for its network, instance, and optional storage resources.
primary_path.success_signal: The new compartment appears in the list and can be selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing IAM permission or invalid compartment details prevent resource organization and creation.

primary_path.action: Open Networking, select Virtual cloud networks, choose Start VCN Wizard, and select Create VCN with Internet Connectivity.
details: The wizard creates a VCN, internet gateway, public and private subnets, route tables, and security lists.
primary_path.success_signal: The VCN configuration form opens for the selected compartment.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing networking permission or wrong compartment prevents VCN setup.

primary_path.action: Name the VCN, keep the tutorial defaults for DNS and public and private subnet address settings, review the selections, and select Create.
primary_path.input: VCN name, compartment, and documented default network settings.
primary_path.success_signal: OCI begins creating the VCN and its related resources.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid CIDR, quota, permission, name, or service errors stop network creation.

primary_path.action: Wait for VCN creation to complete and select View VCN.
details: The tutorial says network creation might take a moment.
primary_path.output: Available internet-connected VCN with public subnet.
primary_path.success_signal: The VCN details page shows the created network resources.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Failed or incomplete related resources prevent the instance from receiving internet connectivity.

primary_path.action: Open Compute, select Instances and Create Instance, then enter the instance name and select the tutorial compartment.
primary_path.output: Create compute instance page scoped to the compartment.
primary_path.success_signal: The instance configuration form accepts the name and compartment.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Missing compute permission or invalid compartment selection prevents configuration.

primary_path.action: Keep the tutorial placement and security defaults, select the Oracle Linux image and an Always Free eligible virtual-machine shape, then select the created VCN and its public subnet with a public IPv4 address.
details: The tutorial illustrates Oracle Linux 8 and an Ampere VM.Standard.A1.Flex shape while noting that actual values can differ.
primary_path.input: Available image, eligible shape, VCN, public subnet, and capacity.
primary_path.output: Configured Oracle Linux VM with public networking.
primary_path.success_signal: The form shows the selected image, shape, VCN, public subnet, and public IPv4 assignment.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Region capacity, shape eligibility, image, networking, quota, or permission problems block creation.

primary_path.action: Use Generate a key pair for me, save both private and public SSH keys in a secure location, keep the documented boot-volume and no-block-volume settings, and select Create.
details: OCI-generated private keys cannot be retrieved again after instance creation.
primary_path.input: Completed instance configuration and local secure key location.
primary_path.output: Saved SSH key pair and submitted instance creation request.
primary_path.success_signal: The instance details page opens in Provisioning state.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Losing the private key, failing to save it, or an instance validation error prevents later connection.

primary_path.action: Wait without refreshing until the instance reaches Running, then allow several additional minutes for Oracle Linux to boot and obtain its public IP address.
details: The tutorial says provisioning takes several minutes and the operating system needs another few minutes before connection.
primary_path.output: Running Oracle Linux instance and public IP address.
primary_path.success_signal: The lifecycle state is Running and the public IP is visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Provisioning, capacity, network, or boot failure prevents SSH access.

primary_path.action: Restrict the private key permissions, then run `ssh -i <private_key_file> opc@<public-ip-address>` and accept the host-key fingerprint on first connection.
details: The Unix-style path uses `chmod 400 <private_key_file>` before SSH; Oracle Linux uses the default username `opc`.
primary_path.input: Saved private key, public IP, local SSH client, and reachable port 22.
primary_path.output: Authenticated SSH connection to the instance.
primary_path.success_signal: SSH accepts the key and opens the remote session.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wrong key permissions, username, key, public IP, network path, boot state, or host-key response blocks connection.

primary_path.action: Verify that the default Oracle Linux shell prompt is visible on the remote instance.
details: The tutorial's optional block-volume and cleanup tasks occur after this first connected-instance result.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer is connected to the default shell for the instance.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: No remote shell means the documented first Linux instance journey is incomplete.

branches.condition: A Linux compute instance is selected.
branches.path: Follow OCI's console tutorial to choose a compartment and image, configure networking and keys, create the instance, wait for its lifecycle state, and connect as documented.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Success becomes a launched and accessible compute instance.

branches.path: Meet CLI requirements, choose or create a compartment, obtain tenancy and compartment identifiers, create a VCN, and issue the documented instance-launch commands until the returned resource state can be verified.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The path adds local CLI installation, credential, identifier, networking, and command-line gates.

friction_gates.description: Email verification is mandatory and its signup link expires after 30 minutes.
Home Region must be selected during signup and cannot be changed later.
Most users must provide a payment card, and a temporary verification hold may appear.
Tenancy services must be provisioned before the OCI Console redirect and welcome email.
OCI requires a resource and interface choice among several equally presented first-resource paths.

sources.title: Get Started with the Command Line Interface
Sign Up for the Oracle Cloud Free Trial
sections_used: Sign Up for the Free Trial
evidence_supported: Address, phone, card, and temporary hold
Tenancy provisioning, Console redirect, and welcome email

sources.title: Oracle Cloud Free Tier
evidence_supported: Phone and credit-card requirement for most users

sources.title: Launching Your First Linux Instance
evidence_supported: Free Tier or paid account and OpenSSH prerequisites
Oracle Linux and Always Free eligible shape configuration
Public IPv4 and generated SSH keys
SSH connection and visible default shell terminal

uncertainties.question: Which OCI resource and interface should represent the platform's first mile?
uncertainties.impact: The full steps, credentials, cost exposure, wait states, and success signal differ by resource.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: OCI officially presents several first-resource tutorials without a single default.

excluded_after_success: No after-success scope is selected while the platform-wide boundary remains unresolved.
Production network design, IAM hardening, scaling, monitoring, and later service tutorials are excluded.
